Header.title: SCARA HMI
Header.networkStatusTooltip: Network Status: Indicates if the web page is currently connected to the network.
Header.connectTooltip: Connect: Opens the serial communication channel using the Web Serial API.
Header.disconnectTooltip: Disconnect: Closes the serial communication channel.
Header.resumeTooltip: RESUME: Clears the E-STOP state and re-enables motor outputs.
Header.stopTooltip: EMERGENCY STOP: Instantly halts all trajectory movements and cuts power to the joint motors.

Login.title: SCARA HMI
Login.subtitle: Research Dashboard
Login.cardDescription: Authentication is required to save experiment runs to the database and access the research dashboard.
Login.footerDisclaimer: Only authorized Google accounts can save experiment data. The HMI itself works without sign-in.
Login.backLink: Back to HMI without signing in

ControlPanel.moveTargetRejected: Move Target Rejected: Target ({x}, {y}) is outside the reachable workspace ({inner} to {outer} mm, -30 to 210 deg).
ControlPanel.trajectoryUnsafe: Move Target Rejected: Trajectory path is unsafe or goes out of bounds.
ControlPanel.tooltipX: Target X coordinate of the end-effector (mm)
ControlPanel.tooltipY: Target Y coordinate of the end-effector (mm)
ControlPanel.kpTooltip: Proportional Gain: Determines responsiveness to current position error. Higher values speed up response but can cause overshoot.
ControlPanel.kiTooltip: Integral Gain: Accumulates positioning error over time to eliminate steady-state offset and keep accuracy high.
ControlPanel.ki2Tooltip: Stepper J2 only requires P+D control to correct the residual CTC trajectory tracking error. Integral gain is disabled to prevent stepper saturation/integration windup.
ControlPanel.kdTooltip: Derivative Gain: Predicts future error by reacting to rate of change, damping oscillations and stabilizing motion.
ControlPanel.ffInertiaTooltip: FF Inertia Blend: Coefficient for Computed Torque Control Inertia feedforward term (0.0 to 1.0).
ControlPanel.ffCoriolisTooltip: FF Coriolis Blend: Coefficient for Computed Torque Control Coriolis/Centrifugal feedforward term (0.0 to 1.0).
ControlPanel.ffGravityTooltip: FF Gravity Blend: Coefficient for Computed Torque Control Gravity feedforward term (0.0 to 1.0).
ControlPanel.microstepTooltip: Microstep: Subdivides motor steps into smaller increments for smoother movements, lower noise, and higher precision.

Tutorial.actionRequired: ⚡ Action required: Perform this action on the HMI to advance automatically, or click skip/simulate below.

step0.title: Welcome to SCARA HMI!
step0.description: This interactive onboarding tour will guide you through the interface controls and walk you through executing your very first trajectory move command!

step1.title: Connect SCARA Robot
step1.description: Establish serial communication with the SCARA arm controller via the Web Serial API. Click this button to select a COM port and connect your hardware.

step2.title: Settings & Diagnostics Menu
step2.description: Configure angular units (radians or degrees), adjust the transparency of previous run ghost trails, customize keyboard keybindings, and download consolidated zip archives containing all 20+ diagnostic graphs and raw CSV telemetry.

step3.title: Step 1: Enter Pick Mode
step3.description: Let's set up your first move! Click the highlighted Pick Point button in the header of the XY Trace panel to enter selection mode.

step4.title: Step 2: Select Target Coordinate
step4.description: Click anywhere on the dark grid area within the reachable workspace boundaries (inside the blue arcs) to select your target coordinates.

step5.title: Step 3: Verify Inputs & Joint Settings
step5.description: Notice that your selected coordinates are now loaded into the Xf and Yf fields. Here you can also adjust PID gains for J1/J2 and CTC feedforward blends.

step6.title: Step 4: Execute Trajectory
step6.description: Now, click the ▶ Run button (or click Next below to simulate) to execute your SCARA move command!

step7.title: Live Serial Terminal
step7.description: Open a real-time monitor panel at the bottom of your screen to inspect raw microcontroller exchanges, commands sent, and telemetry packets.

step8.title: Emergency Stop & Resume
step8.description: Instantly cut power to motors and halt trajectory execution in an emergency. Once safe, click RESUME to clear the stop state and restore active control.

step9.title: Onboarding Complete! 🎉
step9.description: Fantastic job! You've completed the tour, set targets, and executed a SCARA robot trajectory. Feel free to inspect the charts on the right and adjust motor parameters anytime!

AdvTuner.title: Advanced Controller Parameters
AdvTuner.subtitle: Tune trajectory kinematics limits, deadbands, alphas, and hold modes directly on the ESP32.
AdvTuner.disconnected: Disconnected: Connect serial port to sync and tune parameters.
AdvTuner.offlineWarning: Displaying offline defaults. Parameters are not yet synchronized with the hardware. Connect serial and click Sync to fetch current parameters.
AdvTuner.vmaxTooltip: Maximum Cartesian line velocity in m/s (clamped in trajectory planner).
AdvTuner.ddthTooltip: Maximum clamping limit for joint acceleration in rad/s² for system safety.
AdvTuner.cfreqTooltip: Execution frequency of the controller loop on ESP32 in Hz.
AdvTuner.idecayTooltip: Decay coefficient (0.0 to 1.0) applied to the Joint 1 integrator term to prevent integral windup.
AdvTuner.u1maxTooltip: Maximum clamped output value for Joint 1 controller output torque (PWM / Saturation Limit).
AdvTuner.kv1Tooltip: Velocity feedforward gain (fraction per rad/s). Applies vff = kv1 * desired_velocity (dTheta1_d).
AdvTuner.vffmaxTooltip: Maximum absolute fraction for velocity feedforward (fraction of U1_MAX).
AdvTuner.vffdvTooltip: Maximum per-tick change for vff (fraction of U1_MAX).
AdvTuner.taunomTooltip: Normalization parameter for feedforward scaling on Joint 1 Computed Torque Control.
AdvTuner.m22refTooltip: Normalization parameter for inertia feedforward scaling on Joint 2 Computed Torque Control.
AdvTuner.tdDesc: TD replaces the IIR filter. r = bandwidth (rad/s) — larger is more responsive but noisier. h = step size = 3×DT (read-only, determined by firmware).
AdvTuner.td1rTooltip: Bandwidth parameter r for Joint 1 Tracking Differentiator (rad/s). Larger = more responsive, smaller = smoother.
AdvTuner.td2rTooltip: Bandwidth parameter r for Joint 2 Tracking Differentiator (rad/s).
AdvTuner.tdStepTooltip: Step size h = 3×DT. Read-only — automatically calculated by firmware based on loop frequency (cfreq). Cannot be modified directly.
AdvTuner.pwmDbTooltip: Friction deadband threshold (PWM units 0-254) below which motor won't rotate.
AdvTuner.errdzTooltip: Error below this threshold (rad) is treated as zero — prevents pot noise from feeding the integrator and triggering micro-corrections the motor cannot execute through the deadband.
AdvTuner.ifreezeTooltip: When |e1| is below this (rad) and motor is active, the integrator decays instead of accumulating. Prevents I-term from winding up through the deadband on its own and triggering a kick → overshoot → jitter cycle near setpoint.
AdvTuner.fztTooltip: Fractional zero threshold: control effort below this fraction of U1_MAX is treated as zero → PWM output = 0. Prevents motor chatter from noise-floor signals.
AdvTuner.fztkTooltip: Kickstart fractional threshold expressed as a fraction of fzt (e.g., 0.10 = 10%). Applied while trajectory acceleration if enabled.
AdvTuner.kspenTooltip: Toggle reduced fractional threshold during trajectory acceleration.
AdvTuner.dbmenTooltip: When enabled, PWM deadband amplitude is scaled while moving (dbEngageScale).
AdvTuner.dbensTooltip: Scale applied to the computed PWM deadband during movement (0.1 - 1.0).
AdvTuner.dbenTooltip: Position error threshold (radians) below which Joint 1 enters Hold Mode.
AdvTuner.dbrelTooltip: Position error threshold (radians) above which Joint 1 exits/releases Hold Mode.
AdvTuner.dbvelTooltip: Joint velocity threshold (rad/s) below which Joint 1 enters Hold Mode.
AdvTuner.hskpTooltip: Proportional gain (Kp) multiplier scaling factor applied in Hold Mode.
AdvTuner.hskdTooltip: Derivative gain (Kd) multiplier scaling factor applied in Hold Mode.
AdvTuner.db2enTooltip: Position error threshold (radians) below which Joint 2 stepper enters Hold Mode (disables pulses).
AdvTuner.db2relTooltip: Position error threshold (radians) above which Joint 2 stepper exits Hold Mode (reactivates control).
AdvTuner.queueNote: Next trajectory starts automatically once current settles.
AdvTuner.ki2gTooltip: Activation gate for Joint 2 integral action (radians).

AdvancedAnalysis.fftNote: Peaks above ~10 Hz are typically potentiometer noise, not structural dynamics. (X axis is frequency bin — scale by tick rate when known.)
AdvancedAnalysis.noTelemetry: No telemetry — run a move to capture data
AdvancedAnalysis.controlEffortEmpty: No control effort telemetry — run a move to capture data
AdvancedAnalysis.ctcTorqueEmpty: No Computed Torque Control telemetry — run a move with CTC enabled to capture data
AdvancedAnalysis.j1PidEmpty: No PID effort breakdown telemetry — run a move with detail logging to capture data
AdvancedAnalysis.esp32LoopEmpty: No loop duration telemetry — run a move with detail logging to capture data

AnalysisTab.copilotAdvisorDesc: Run a move and select <bold>Run + Save to DB</bold> to consult the AI Copilot regarding your controller parameters and telemetry.
AnalysisTab.copilotReadyDesc: Choose your mode and click Consult to stream live suggestions, explanation, or root cause diagnoses based on your telemetry.
AnalysisTab.j1VelocityPotTd: J1 Velocity Estimation: Pot/TD vs. Encoder (Temporary)
AnalysisTab.expandFullscreen: Expand to fullscreen. Press ESC to exit.

CaptureMenu.panelDesc: Configure angular units, manage workspace overlays, and customize diagnostic exporters.
CaptureMenu.helpOnboardingDesc: Need help navigating the SCARA workspace? Replay the step-by-step interactive onboarding tour.
CaptureMenu.unitDesc: Sets the angular position and velocity unit mappings across both Chart dashboards.
CaptureMenu.ghostDesc: Adjusts the canvas transparency for previous run trajectory overlays.
CaptureMenu.noTrajectoryData: No trajectory data is loaded. Run a coordinate move command on the dashboard first to unlock diagnostics packaging.
CaptureMenu.captureAllCsvDesc: Bundles 20 images and raw spreadsheet CSV
CaptureMenu.prefixDesc: Letters, numbers, dashes, and underscores only.

ChartPanel.collapseDesc: Collapse: Restores the panel to normal size.
ChartPanel.calipersInstructions: Select Calipers tool and click at two distinct times along the horizontal axis to measure settling oscillations or overshoot ratios.
ChartPanel.msgNoPid: No PID telemetry — run a move to capture data
ChartPanel.msgNoJ1Ctrl: No J1 control telemetry — run a move to capture data (requires updated firmware)
ChartPanel.msgNoJ2Vel: No J2 velocity telemetry — run a move to capture data
ChartPanel.msgNoEefErr: No end-effector error telemetry — run a move to capture data
ChartPanel.msgNoCte: No CTE telemetry — run a move to capture data
ChartPanel.msgNoAte: No ATE telemetry — run a move to capture data
ChartPanel.msgNoEefVel: No end-effector velocity telemetry — run a move to capture data
ChartPanel.msgNoPwm: No PWM telemetry — run a move to capture data
ChartPanel.msgNoPos: No position telemetry — run a move to capture data
ChartPanel.msgNoVel: No velocity telemetry — run a move to capture data

runMetrics.title: RUN METRICS
runMetrics.aiTooltip: Accuracy Index (AI): 1 − MCTE/D. 100% = perfect path tracking.
runMetrics.epsMaxTooltip: Maximum Cross Tracking Error (ε_max): worst lateral deviation in the run.
runMetrics.mcteTooltip: Mean CTE (MCTE): path-integrated lateral area divided by path length.
runMetrics.rmsAteTooltip: RMS Along-Track Error: quadratic average of lead/lag without sign cancellation.
runMetrics.reTooltip: Error Bias (R_ε): >50% Delay → raise Kp/Ki/Kff; >50% Shape → tune Kd or check mechanics.
runMetrics.epsFinalTooltip: Final CTE (ε_f): cross-track error at the end of the trajectory.
runMetrics.telTooltip: Elapsed time (T_el): total duration of the last trajectory run.
runMetrics.rmseJ1Tooltip: Joint 1 tracking RMSE (°): root-mean-square of θ1 position error over the run.
runMetrics.rmseJ2Tooltip: Joint 2 tracking RMSE (°): root-mean-square of θ2 position error over the run.
runMetrics.rmseEefTooltip: End-effector RMSE (mm): root-mean-square of Cartesian EEF position error over the run.
runMetrics.ctrlVarTooltip: Control Effort Variance (PWM σ²): higher values indicate more active correction. Very high values may indicate oscillation.
runMetrics.jitterTooltip: Actuator Jitter Proxy (mean |ΔPWM| per step): indicator of chattering. Lower is smoother.

SerialLog.filteredDesc: Filtered: Hiding high-frequency telemetry packets (D/T/F/E/B). Click to show all.
SerialLog.unfilteredDesc: Unfiltered: Showing all raw serial data. Click to hide telemetry noise.
SerialLog.clearLogDesc: Clear Log: Removes all entries from the serial terminal display.
SerialLog.clearGraphDesc: Clear Graph: Resets trajectory data buffers and clears all chart plots.

SerialTerminal.tooltipClose: Serial Monitor: Click to close the terminal panel. (ESC also closes it.)
SerialTerminal.tooltipOpen: Serial Monitor: Opens a live serial log panel at the bottom of the screen.

XYTrace.title: XY Trace
XYTrace.recordingTooltip: Recording: Telemetry capture is active and drawing live trajectory path.
XYTrace.idleTooltip: Idle: Telemetry recording is completed. Displaying last move.
XYTrace.waitingTooltip: Waiting: Standing by for a move command.
XYTrace.pickPointCancelTooltip: Cancel: Exit target coordinate selection mode.
XYTrace.pickPointTooltip: Pick Point: Click a point on the workspace graph to set it as target coordinates.
XYTrace.ghostModeTooltip: Ghost Mode: Toggle overlay of the previous trajectory paths.
XYTrace.armLinksTooltip: Arm Links: Toggle physical SCARA arm link visualization.
XYTrace.collapseTooltip: Collapse: Restores the panel to normal size.
XYTrace.pickPointBanner: Click workspace to set target point (Esc to exit)

DashboardGroupCompareTab.title: Statistical Group Comparison
DashboardGroupCompareTab.unassignedWarning: {count} selected runs are not assigned to any subgroup of this group.
DashboardGroupCompareTab.unloadedWarning: {count} runs assigned in this group configuration are not currently selected in the sidebar.
DashboardGroupCompareTab.noAssignedRunsWarning: This group has no assigned runs.
DashboardGroupCompareTab.noAssignedRunsDesc: Open the **Group Editor** to add subgroups and assign runs loaded from the sidebar.

mcte_cruise.label: MCTE Cruise
mcte_cruise.desc: Mean CTE during Cruise (ta <= t < ta + tc)

mcte_decel.label: MCTE Decel
mcte_decel.desc: Mean CTE during Deceleration (ta + tc <= t < tf)

mate_cruise.label: MATE Cruise
mate_cruise.desc: Mean |ATE| during Cruise (ta <= t < ta + tc)

mate_decel.label: MATE Decel
mate_decel.desc: Mean |ATE| during Deceleration (ta + tc <= t < tf)

ssErr.label: Steady State Error
ssErr.desc: Cartesian error at final trajectory point

DashboardGroupCompareTab.distributionDesc: Hover on circles to view run details or boxes to inspect quantiles (whiskers: min/max, boxes: Q1/Q3).
DashboardGroupCompareTab.relativeTimeDesc: Resampled telemetry runs aligned relatively to $t_0=0$ and averaged within subgroups. Final values held constant (steady-state clamp).
DashboardGroupCompareTab.jointTrajectoriesDesc: Select a subgroup below to view actual vs desired tracking trends for Joint 1 and Joint 2.
DashboardGroupCompareTab.noRunsForSubgroup: No runs assigned or selected for this subgroup.
DashboardGroupCompareTab.welchDesc: Check if differences between two subgroups are statistically significant (Welch's t-test handles unequal sample sizes and variances).
DashboardGroupCompareTab.selectTwoSubgroups: Select two different subgroups to run t-tests.
DashboardGroupCompareTab.subgroupsMustHaveRuns: Both subgroups must have at least one loaded run to perform analysis.
DashboardGroupCompareTab.refGuideWelch: computes the probability (p-value) that the difference between means is due to random chance. A {code} (Significant) indicates less than 5% probability, validating a true performance difference.
DashboardGroupCompareTab.refGuideCohen: measures the effect size (standardized difference). A value {code} indicates a **Large** effect, showing a highly practical difference in control regimes.
DashboardGroupCompareTab.geometricalDesc: Average trajectory coordinate traces (solid lines) plotted on the Cartesian XY plane, surrounded by a 1-standard-deviation confidence corridor (shaded bands) representing geometric spread.
DashboardGroupCompareTab.understandingP1: The dashed line represents the {desired}. The solid colored lines represent the {average} followed by each subgroup.
DashboardGroupCompareTab.understandingP2: The shaded area surrounding each line is the {corridor}:
DashboardGroupCompareTab.understandingP3: where {sigma} is the standard deviation of lateral (cross-track) error at position {s}, and {n} is the normal vector perpendicular to the desired path.
DashboardGroupCompareTab.understandingP4: This visualizes {reproducibility}. A larger offset of the average line from the desired path indicates persistent tracking errors (e.g. friction or dynamics lag), while a wider shaded band indicates higher run-to-run variation.
